BackstoryDef.title: nomad
BackstoryDef.description: [PAWN_nameDef]'s parents were wanderers. They didn't belong to any tribe and travelled from village to village. [PAWN_pronoun] learned to forage for food and followed them on fishing and hunting trips when winter was harsh. Unfortunately, [PAWN_pronoun] never really socialized with other children, and preferred to stay with [PAWN_possessive] parents whenever they were staying in a settlement.

BackstoryDef.title: savage
BackstoryDef.description: Found very young in the wild by tribespeople, [PAWN_nameDef]'s origins are unknown. Acting like an animal, [PAWN_pronoun] was taken by a family and taught to talk and interact with fellow tribe members.\n\nIf [PAWN_pronoun] is properly integrated into human society and doesn't remember [PAWN_possessive] time in the wilderness, [PAWN_pronoun] still struggles to socialize and often acts strangely to the eyes of other people.

BackstoryDef.title: pebble painter
BackstoryDef.description: [PAWN_nameDef] loved to paint rocks. [PAWN_pronoun] collected pebbles and used various dyes to create small images on their surface. [PAWN_pronoun] kept [PAWN_possessive] best works in [PAWN_possessive] collection, safe from the other kids that might want to steal one.

BackstoryDef.title: tender
BackstoryDef.description: Learning from [PAWN_possessive] parents, [PAWN_nameDef] helped them heal injured warriors or sick people. [PAWN_pronoun] cleaned the wounds and sew them close again, or prepared various infusions.\n\nDeaths occured frequently, and each time, [PAWN_nameDef] couldn't chase the sad thoughts from [PAWN_possessive] mind.

BackstoryDef.title: shaman apprentice
BackstoryDef.description: [PAWN_nameDef] was found very young in the wilderness and was taken by a local tribe. People never trusted [PAWN_objective] and believed [PAWN_pronoun] was some sort of monstrous night creature in hiding. [PAWN_pronoun] was eventually adopted and raised by a shaman, from which [PAWN_pronoun] learned to mix herbs and talk to the spirits.
